qualifying_org_definition: Any nonprofit, government agency, or organization where the ABAWD performs volunteer/community service work; no pre-approved list or MOU required for the general volunteer lane (MOU requirement applies only to workfare sites, not general community service).
verification_instrument: CF 888 (CalFresh Volunteer Work Verification Form) — signed by an authorized representative of the volunteer organization; county may also accept a verbal statement from an org representative if the form cannot be obtained.
hours_formula_detail: 80 hours/month (or average 20 hours/week) per 7 CFR 273.24 and confirmed by CA state and county guidance; workfare uses allotment/min-wage but the general volunteer lane uses the standard 80-hour federal baseline.
accepts_outofstate_detail: No CA state or county source reviewed explicitly restricts volunteer host organizations to California-based entities. CDSS policy, WCLP, LSNC calfresh.guide, Santa Clara County handbook, and five county pages describe qualifying orgs only by type (nonprofit, community service org, government agency) with no in-state location requirement. Federal default under 7 CFR 273.24 accepts any qualifying nonprofit regardless of location; absent an explicit CA restriction, out-of-state nonprofits operating remotely or in-person in CA should be accepted operationally.
geographic_waivers: Seven counties waived November 1, 2025 – October 31, 2026: Colusa, Imperial, Tulare, Alpine, Merced, Monterey, and Plumas. All other 51 counties fully enforced starting June 1, 2026 (statewide waiver ended November 1, 2025; state chose June 1, 2026 as implementation date using a fixed 36-month clock beginning January 1, 2026).
submission_deadline: Verified at next recertification (periodic report or renewal); mid-period reporting required within 10 days if hours drop below 80/month (20/week). No fixed monthly filing deadline.
notes:
California is Tier 1 clean: the general volunteer/community service lane requires no county assignment, EDP, or MOU — the MOU requirement applies only to workfare sites, not to nonprofits certifying general volunteer hours. Verification is via the CF 888 form signed by the nonprofit's authorized representative (or a verbal statement if the form is unavailable), making this a direct nonprofit-to-recipient certification. county_variation_flag is set true because California administers CalFresh at the county level and county implementation of the ABAWD work requirement (enforced starting June 1, 2026 as the statewide waiver ended) may vary in practice, though state policy establishes the clean lane.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Conducted an adversarial multi-source search specifically attempting to find hidden presteps (EDP, work registration, county site approval, MOU) that would refute the Tier 1 classification. Searched: (1) 'CalFresh ABAWD work registration intake requirement county 2026' — confirmed work registration and ABAWD requirements are explicitly two separate rule sets; work registration is NOT a gating prestep for the volunteer lane. (2) 'California SNAP E&T State Plan FY2026 volunteer community service track' — CalFresh E&T is optional at the county level; counties choose whether to offer it; the general volunteer lane is independent of E&T enrollment. (3) 'CalFresh ABAWD approved volunteer site list nonprofit registration 2025 2026' — no statewide approved-site list found; MOU requirement confirmed to apply only to workfare sites. (4) Multiple county pages fetched directly: Alameda, Napa, Solano, LA County (DPSS), Santa Clara — none describe an EDP, case manager assignment, or county pre-approval as a prerequisite for general volunteer hours. (5) Santa Clara County ABAWD handbook (StsfygABAWDWkReq.htm) is the most explicit source: 'Community service or volunteer hours performed at any other non-workfare site can be used to satisfy the work requirement' with no MOU required; the CF 888 (or verbal statement) is the verification instrument. (6) WCLP (Western Center on Law & Poverty) and LSNC calfresh.guide resources — neither describes any mandatory EDP, work registration, or county site pre-approval for the volunteer lane. (7) Santa Clara County 'Initial Determination' handbook page confirms the county worker only 'encourages' volunteer options at intake; this is informational, not a gatekeeping step. (8) CalFresh Update 2025-07 confirms CF 888 is 'optional' as a form — county can also accept verbal statement from the org — reinforcing the low-barrier self-reported model. No evidence found across any source of a required prestep that would move this record to Tier 2. Confidence upheld at 'high' based on: state CDSS policy page, Santa Clara County operational handbook (most detailed public source), WCLP legal aid analysis, LSNC guide, and five county-level pages all consistently describing no mandatory EDP/registration/site-approval gating. Remaining gap: 'remote_stance' stays 'unknown' — no public source addresses whether remote/online volunteer hours qualify; this does not affect the tier classification but is a gap for product guidance.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) HOURS: Federal 80-hour/month baseline confirmed by Santa Clara County handbook, Napa County, Solano County, LSNC guide, and WCLP — all consistently cite '80 hours per month or 20 hours per week.' Workfare uses the allotment/min-wage formula but that lane is separate; the volunteer/community-service lane uses the standard 80-hour federal floor. (2) OUT-OF-STATE NONPROFIT: All sources reviewed are silent on org location. CDSS ABAWD page, county pages, WCLP, LSNC guide, and Santa Clara County handbook describe qualifying orgs by type only (nonprofit, community org, government agency) with no California-only residency requirement for the host org. Federal default under 7 CFR 273.24 accepts any qualifying nonprofit, so 'silent' is the correct coding; operationally Tended should be acceptable as a CA-based 501(c)(3) signing CF 888s. (3) GEOGRAPHIC WAIVERS: Statewide waiver ended November 1, 2025. Seven counties retain individual waivers Nov 1, 2025–Oct 31, 2026 (Colusa, Imperial, Tulare, Alpine, Merced, Monterey, Plumas), per LSNC waiver page. All remaining 51 counties are enforcing as of June 1, 2026. (4) SUBMISSION UX: Food Bank of Contra Costa and Solano documentation (foodbankccs.org/abawd) describes three mechanisms: BenefitsCal online upload, mail, or in-person to county office. Coded as online_portal + mail + paper_county_office. Reporting cadence is at_recertification (verified at next periodic report or renewal), with a mid-period obligation to report within 10 days if hours fall below threshold — no fixed monthly filing deadline found in any source. (5) FORM FIELDS: The CF 888 has Section 1 (recipient) and Section 2 (org/activity). Confirmed fields from multiple source descriptions: recipient name, recipient case number; org name, org address, org phone, authorized representative signature, supervisor name; total hours, hours by day/week breakdown, activity description, period start/end dates. No notarization requirement found. EIN not confirmed on the form — no source cited it. Coded conservatively without EIN or notarization.
